Name: hardware_optimized_inference
Title: Hardware-Optimized Inference for Efficient Neural Network Deployment
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging hardware-aware optimization techniques, such as quantization, sparsity, and knowledge distillation, we can significantly improve the efficiency of neural network inference on commodity GPUs, reducing latency and increasing throughput while maintaining accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works have explored various optimization techniques for efficient neural network inference, including quantization [1], sparsity [2], and knowledge distillation [3]. However, these methods often rely on theoretical bounds or simulation-based evaluations, which may not accurately reflect real-world performance. In contrast, our proposal focuses on hardware-aware optimization, leveraging profiling and benchmarking to optimize neural network inference on commodity GPUs.
Abstract: This proposal presents a hardware-optimized approach to efficient neural network inference on commodity GPUs. By combining quantization, sparsity, and knowledge distillation with hardware-aware optimization techniques, we aim to reduce latency and increase throughput while maintaining accuracy. Our approach leverages profiling and benchmarking to optimize neural network inference, ensuring that our optimizations are tailored to the specific hardware and use case. We evaluate our approach on a range of benchmarks, including image classification and natural language processing tasks, and demonstrate significant improvements in efficiency and performance.
Experiments: Quantization-aware training and inference on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets
Sparsity-aware pruning and knowledge distillation on BERT and RoBERTa models
Hardware-aware optimization of neural network inference on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs
Evaluation of latency and throughput on single-GPU and multi-GPU setups
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential accuracy degradation due to quantization and sparsity
Increased complexity of hardware-aware optimization techniques
Dependence on specific hardware and software configurations
